workflow.persona: Internal product strategist mapping the workflow used by an outsourced compliance provider
workflow.entry_condition: A draft advertisement or promotional communication is ready for compliance review before publication.
workflow.completion_condition: The item is approved or rejected, the final review package is archived, and monitoring follow-up is queued.
workflow_notes: This first slice models one workflow only.
Representative internal artifacts are included as workflow inputs, not as fabricated evidence.
Source evidence is limited to cited SEC guidance, risk alerts, examination priorities, and enforcement releases.

sources.title: Investment Adviser Marketing
sources.summary: Summarizes the marketing rule's general prohibitions plus conditions for testimonials, endorsements, third-party ratings, and performance advertising.

sources.title: Examinations Focused on the New Investment Adviser Marketing Rule
sources.summary: Explains that examinations will focus on policies and procedures, substantiation, performance advertising, and books and records under the new rule.

sources.title: Examinations Focused on Additional Areas of the Adviser Marketing Rule
sources.summary: Adds testimonials, endorsements, third-party ratings, and Form ADV alignment to the examination focus areas.

sources.title: Initial Observations Regarding Advisers Act Marketing Rule Compliance
sources.summary: Describes observed deficiencies around unsubstantiated statements, omissions, misleading inferences, unbalanced presentations, books and records, and compliance program gaps.

sources.title: SEC Charges Five Investment Advisers for Marketing Rule Violations
sources.summary: Shows the SEC enforcing hypothetical-performance requirements where advisers advertised to the public without the required policies and procedures.

sources.title: SEC Charges Nine Investment Advisers in Ongoing Sweep into Marketing Rule Violations
sources.summary: Shows active enforcement for untrue or unsubstantiated statements plus testimonials, endorsements, and third-party ratings that lacked required disclosures.

sources.title: SEC Division of Examinations Announces 2026 Priorities
sources.summary: Signals that advisers should strengthen compliance programs in core areas and be ready for constructive supervisory dialogue in examinations.

artifacts.name: Draft ad copy
artifacts.description: The proposed text, image, or landing-page copy submitted for review.

artifacts.name: Channel and audience plan
artifacts.description: Planned distribution channel, intended audience, campaign context, and publication date.

artifacts.name: Campaign brief
artifacts.description: Business objective, target message, and any claims the campaign intends to make.

artifacts.name: Promoter, testimonial, and third-party rating inventory
artifacts.description: List of testimonials, endorsements, ratings, compensation terms, and conflicts that may trigger disclosures or agreements.

artifacts.name: Performance support workbook
artifacts.description: Supporting calculations, assumptions, and intended-audience notes for any performance or hypothetical performance content.

artifacts.name: Substantiation memo
artifacts.description: Short memo linking each material statement of fact to support and noting any unresolved gaps.

artifacts.name: Disclosure matrix
artifacts.description: Checklist for required disclosures tied to testimonials, ratings, performance, and other ad claims.

artifacts.name: Compliance review memo
artifacts.description: Issue list, redlines, and remediation asks provided back to marketing or the outsourced provider.

artifacts.name: Approval log and supervisor attestation
artifacts.description: Recorded approval decision, approver identity, date, and any conditions placed on publication.

artifacts.name: Archive bundle and published capture
artifacts.description: Final approved materials, supporting documentation, published screenshot or capture, and retention metadata.

steps.step_description: Collect the draft content, channel plan, intended audience, attachments, and supporting files into one normalized review packet before any substantive review begins.

automation_fit.rationale: The work is structured intake and packet assembly with stable required fields.

human_signoff_need.rationale: A person can review exceptions, but the packet-building step itself does not need final human judgment.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: Errors are usually caught downstream before publication, so the main risk is process friction rather than irreversible harm.

evidence_strength.rationale: The intake checklist can be anchored to explicit rule-review inputs, source documents, and books-and-records expectations.

evidence_notes.claim: The review starts from identifiable advertisement conditions and content categories, which supports a structured intake packet.
Examiners review policies, procedures, and books and records, so consistent packet assembly reduces readiness gaps.
The priorities push firms toward stronger baseline compliance programs, which favors standardized intake over ad hoc email routing.

unknowns: Firm-specific routing fields may vary by channel or outside counsel requirements.
steps.build_implication: Good automate candidate: normalize intake and create a review-ready bundle before a human reviewer opens the file.

steps.step_name: Classify whether the item is an advertisement
steps.step_description: Determine whether the communication falls inside the rule and whether any exclusions, audience nuances, or channel-specific interpretations affect the review path.

automation_fit.rationale: The step can use checklists and pattern matching, but facts and circumstances still matter.

human_signoff_need.rationale: Classification errors change the whole review path and should be confirmed by a human.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: A wrong classification can send the piece through the wrong controls and create downstream exposure.

evidence_strength.rationale: The rule text and exam alerts provide real anchors, but edge cases still depend on interpretation.

evidence_notes.claim: The guide lays out the rule's prohibited practices and content conditions, which gives a starting classification frame.
The SEC signaled that advisers should update policies and procedures around the new rule, which implies classification logic must be defensible.

unknowns: Some firms treat social fragments, event remarks, or linked content with fact-specific overrides.
steps.build_implication: Best handled as an assistive classifier that shows why the item likely is or is not an advertisement.

steps.step_name: Flag testimonials, ratings, and performance content
steps.step_description: Check whether the item includes testimonials, endorsements, third-party ratings, specific investment advice, performance claims, or hypothetical performance that triggers extra conditions.

automation_fit.rationale: Many triggers are pattern-detectable and checklist-driven, especially for known content types.

human_signoff_need.rationale: Humans should confirm the detection result because image context, compensation details, and audience fit can change the answer.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: Missing one trigger can create disclosure failures or unsupported performance exposure.

evidence_strength.rationale: SEC guidance and enforcement both identify the specific content areas that need extra scrutiny.

evidence_notes.claim: The guide spells out conditions for testimonials, endorsements, third-party ratings, and performance information.
The SEC explicitly elevated testimonials, endorsements, third-party ratings, and Form ADV alignment as focus areas.
Recent charges show that missing disclosures and unsubstantiated claims still lead to enforcement.
Hypothetical-performance handling remains an active enforcement topic when audience and policy controls are weak.

unknowns: Creative assets may hide claims inside images or short video captions that require OCR or richer media parsing.
steps.build_implication: Strong assist candidate: automate red-flag detection and disclosure reminders, but keep human confirmation before any approval.

steps.step_description: Gather backing support for each material statement of fact and build the disclosure package needed for any ratings, testimonials, or performance content.

automation_fit.rationale: Evidence collection and disclosure assembly can follow structured templates tied to detected claim types.

human_signoff_need.rationale: Humans still review the final package, but the assembly work itself is mostly operational.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: A bad bundle is still risky, but it is discoverable before publication and can be corrected inside the review loop.

evidence_strength.rationale: This step is directly grounded in substantiation and disclosure expectations described in SEC guidance and enforcement.

evidence_notes.claim: Advisers need a reasonable basis to substantiate material statements and must satisfy disclosure conditions around specific content types.
Observed deficiencies included unsubstantiated statements and unbalanced presentations, making evidence bundling a concrete control point.
The SEC has penalized firms for weak hypothetical-performance controls, which increases the value of structured support packages.
Enforcement tied to missing disclosures reinforces the need for a repeatable disclosure assembly step.

unknowns: Some firms may require outside-counsel review for unusual performance claims.
steps.build_implication: High-value automate wedge: package substantiation evidence and disclosure support before human review.

steps.step_name: Draft the review memo and remediation path
steps.step_description: Summarize issues, explain why the item fails or passes, and drive revisions with marketing or outsourced provider teams until the piece is ready for decision.

automation_fit.rationale: Issue drafting is partly templated, but good remediation advice depends on context and judgment.

human_signoff_need.rationale: A human should decide whether the explanations and fixes truly cure the problem before approval.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: Bad remediation guidance can create a false sense of safety and push a risky ad closer to publication.

evidence_strength.rationale: The memo can cite concrete findings, but balancing language and adequacy judgments remain qualitative.

evidence_notes.claim: Observed deficiencies around omissions, misleading inferences, and unfairly balanced presentations require nuanced reviewer explanations.
The SEC expects stronger compliance programs and supervisory readiness, which favors consistent memos but not autonomous dispositioning.

unknowns: Some firms prefer short issue lists while others require formal memo language and legal-style citations.
steps.build_implication: Assist candidate: draft issue lists, redline suggestions, and remediation checklists, but keep humans in charge of the actual judgment call.

steps.step_name: Record principal or CCO approval for publication
steps.step_description: An authorized compliance leader decides whether the piece can be published, records the basis for the decision, and sets any conditions that must hold at launch.

automation_fit.rationale: This is a supervisory judgment step, not just a checklist action.

human_signoff_need.rationale: The publication decision should stay with a named human decision-maker.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: A bad automated approval can put a noncompliant ad into market and create direct regulatory exposure.

evidence_strength.rationale: There is evidence for why the step matters, but the decision still requires accountable human judgment.

evidence_notes.claim: The examination program emphasizes core compliance readiness, which favors explicit supervisory ownership over autonomous release.
Recent enforcement shows real penalties when advertisements go live with missing disclosures or unsupported statements.
Hypothetical-performance violations illustrate how costly a wrong publication decision can become.

unknowns: Some firms require dual sign-off when performance or promoter compensation is involved.
steps.build_implication: Keep human. Build audit support around this step, not autonomous approval.

steps.step_name: Archive the final package and queue monitoring
steps.step_description: Store the final approval packet, capture the published output, and queue post-publication monitoring or periodic re-review tasks.

automation_fit.rationale: Archival packaging and monitoring queue creation are repetitive, structured, and policy-driven.

human_signoff_need.rationale: Humans should review exceptions, but the default archival path can be system-run.

liability_asymmetry.rationale: The main risk is documentation gaps, which automation can reduce rather than create.

evidence_strength.rationale: Books-and-records expectations and recent enforcement make this step highly evidenceable.

evidence_notes.claim: Books and records are a stated exam area, so archive completeness is a concrete control requirement.
The SEC tied marketing-rule observations back to books-and-records and compliance-rule gaps.
Enforcement actions have included failures to maintain required copies, increasing the value of automated archival capture.

unknowns: Retention schedule and monitoring cadence may vary by firm policy and channel.
steps.build_implication: Strong automate candidate because it is structured, repetitive, and directly improves exam readiness.
